alkylation reagent: 1 μL of 550 mM iodoacetamide in the dark
cleavage agent: digested at room temperature for 3 h with lysyl endopeptidase (Wako)
collision energy: The relative collision energy for rapid collision-induced dissociation (rCID) was set to 35%
fragmentation method: the average identification rate was 22%, which is in the typical range for high-throughput proteomics using CID as fragmentation technique
instrument: analyzed with a Q-Exactive HF mass spectrometer
labeling: MaxQuant 1.5.3.175 label-free protein quantifications (LFQ) algorithm used for quantification
